Government restricts bulk industrial fuel purchases at petrol pumpsThe government has barred industrial, commercial and institutional users from buying petrol and diesel at retail pumps. The move seeks to prevent diversion, curb shortages and protect supplies for ordinary vehicle owners.

India to stay world's fastest-growing major economy, growth at 6.6%: World BankIndia will remain the world's fastest-growing major economy, with growth expected to slow from 7.7% in FY2025-26 to 6.6% in FY2026-27 before recovering to 7.2% the following year.

Why alcohol sales are set to slow globally over next 10 years but grow in IndiaAs global alcohol consumption enters a phase of slowdown, India appears to be charting a very different growth path. What lies behind this contrasting trend?

India overtakes the world's biggest economy in construction growthThe country has been investing heavily in highways, railways, airports, ports, metro systems and logistics networks. These projects are not only creating jobs and boosting economic activity today but are also laying the foundation for future growth.

Is your CIBIL score below 730? RBI's new rules may make loans harder to getThe RBI's Expected Credit Loss rules from April 1, 2027 could make banks stricter with borrowers below a 730 CIBIL score. The shift may raise rates, collateral demands and rejection risks for applicants with thin or irregular credit histories.

8th Pay Commission: Will state government employees get salary hikes too?The 8th Central Pay Commission has been constituted by the Union government to review salaries, pensions, allowances and other service conditions of Central government employees and pensioners.

No E30 at pumps yet, so why has India exempted higher ethanol petrol from tax?India has exempted E22, E25, E27 and E30 petrol blends from excise duty despite no retail rollout yet. The move sets up a policy framework for higher ethanol fuels as questions persist over vehicle compatibility and costs.

Infosys, HCL Tech, TCS fall up to 3%: Why are IT stocks falling today?The Nifty IT index fell 2.67% to 27,525.80, emerging as the worst-performing sector on Dalal Street as of 9:50 am. The decline also weighed heavily on the Sensex, where IT majors were among the biggest losers.

Was Madhuri Dixit's blouse a fight against patriarchy? Maa Behen director answers Suresh Triveni addressed the debate over the sleeveless blouse worn by Madhuri Dixit's character in Maa Behen. He said the garment reflects personal choice within the story, even as viewers read it as a symbol of patriarchy.

Jana Nayagan leak case: Absconding mastermind arrested in Chennai The Tamil Nadu Cyber Crime Wing has arrested the alleged mastermind in the Jana Nayagan footage leak case. The latest arrests have taken the total to 19 as the probe and piracy warning continue.

Rs 1.7 crore for Welcome To The Jungle? Akshay Kumar reacts to fee rumours At the trailer launch of Welcome to the Jungle, Akshay Kumar dismissed reports that he charged Rs 1.7 crore for the film. He said the project matters to him because Neeraj Vora wrote the story.

Live: US intercepts Iranian drones targeting vessels in Hormuz after Trump's ceasefire vowUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The apparent diplomatic breakthrough announced by US President Donald Trump was quickly clouded by uncertainty after Iranian media pushed back against his claims that key points of an agreement had already been approved. Trump said he cancelled planned strikes on Iran because discussions had reached the highest levels of the Iranian leadership and had secured broad regional backing. However, Iran's semi-official Fars News Agency later cited a source close to the negotiating team as saying that Tehran had not approved any text for a preliminary memorandum of understanding with Washington. The conflicting accounts left the true state of negotiations unclear, even as both sides continued indirect contacts. Trump earlier vowed to take Kharg Island, which handles roughly 90% of Iran's crude exports. Plans to capture the island have been drawn up for months but continuously shelved because it was considered too risky. Iranian officials have maintained that the country is prepared for any escalation and have rejected suggestions that military pressure would force concessions. Meanwhile, reports from Iranian and Western officials indicate that negotiations remain active, with major issues such as sanctions relief, frozen Iranian assets and regional security arrangements still unresolved. Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates.

Just made a great settlement: Trump says Iran deal nearly done, signing awaited Live UpdatesDonald Trump said that a signing ceremony could take place in Europe as early as this weekend, although he would not attend personally. Vice President JD Vance is expected to represent the United States at the event.

A Tinder right swipe gone wrong: Haryana judge loses Rs 52 lakh to honeytrapA Haryana judge allegedly transferred more than Rs 52 lakh to a man she met on Tinder, who promised high returns on investments. A Delhi court denied his bail and questioned why the complaint was filed in the name of the judge's domestic worker while key digital evidence remains missing.

3 killed as fire erupts in Delhi house basement, engulfs vehicles, upper floorsThree people, including a man and two women, died while five women were rescued after a massive fire broke out in the basement of a house in Delhi's Tuglakabad area, spreading to parked vehicles and engulfing multiple floors of the building.

Bali employee tells boss he wants to work after bike accident, her reply goes viralIn the WhatsApp conversation, the employee informed his boss that he had fallen off his motorbike and was unsure whether he would be able to come to work the next day.

Did you enjoy that, Pak asylum seeker asks drunk UK teen after raping her; jailedSheraz Malik was jailed for 10 years after a jury found him guilty of raping an 18-year-old woman in a Nottinghamshire park. The court said he targeted a heavily intoxicated teenager, while police are still tracing a second suspect.

Woman doctor issues video apology over male corpses' private parts commentThe woman, identified as Sejal Pawar, came under fire after a clip from Pranit More's show went viral on social media. Addressing the backlash, Pawar said she wanted to publicly acknowledge her mistake and apologise.

World Cup: Hwang In-beom fires South Korea to 2-1 comeback victory over Czechia South Korea produced a stirring comeback to defeat Czechia 2-1 in their FIFA World Cup Group A opener in Guadalajara on Thursday, with Hwang In-beom starring in a memorable second-half turnaround.

England braces for history as Women's T20 World Cup promises a blockbuster The 2026 Women's T20 World Cup promises to be a landmark event, with 12 teams competing in England and Wales. While Australia start as favourites, Harmanpreet Kaur's India, hosts England, defending champions New Zealand and South Africa are expected to be strong contenders.

Mexico's Quinones recreates Tshabalala's 2010 celebration in front of South Africa Julian Quinones turned the clock back 16 years on the opening day of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, recreating Siphiwe Tshabalala's iconic celebration after scoring against South Africa as football's biggest tournament came full circle.

South Africa coach unhappy with red card call after crushing loss vs Mexico FIFA World Cup 2026: South Africa coach Hugo Broos accepted one red card but disputed another after Bafana Bafana's 2-0 defeat to Mexico, insisting his side played better than the scoreline suggested despite finishing the World Cup opener with nine men.

World Cup: Mexico boss Javier Aguirre demands more after victory over South Africa Mexico coach Javier Aguirre said his side had shaken off the nerves that came with opening the FIFA World Cup on home soil after a 2-0 win over nine-man South Africa in Group A at the Estadio Azteca on Thursday, but stressed that Mexico would need to raise their level in the matches ahead.

Wataru Endo retires from Japan duty after injury ends World Cup 2026 dream FIFA World Cup 2026: Wataru Endo has withdrawn from Japan's World Cup squad and retired from international football after injury. His exit leaves Japan without their captain days before the tournament and opens a new leadership question.

Who is Wilton Sampaio? Referee goes viral after 3 red cards on World Cup Day 1 FIFA World Cup 2026: Three red cards, a futuristic headset and countless memes. Brazilian referee Wilton Sampaio became one of the biggest stars of World Cup opening night as Mexico's fiery win over South Africa descended into chaos.

Who is Gilberto Mora? 17-year-old Mexico star breaks 96-year World Cup record Gilberto Mora's FIFA World Cup debut lasted only a few minutes, but it was enough to rewrite Mexican football history. The 17-year-old midfielder became Mexico's youngest-ever World Cup player as El Tri launched their home tournament with a convincing win over South Africa.

Sleepless and streamless: Indian fans face broadcast glitches on World Cup Day 1 FIFA World Cup 2026: Indian football fans stayed up through the night for the return of the FIFA World Cup, only to find themselves battling buffering screens, app crashes and streaming complaints.

3 reds, 2 goals: Mexico sweep South Africa 2-0 in frenetic FIFA World Cup opener Mexico opened the 2026 World Cup with a 2-0 win over South Africa in a match marred by three red cards. The result gave the co-hosts early control in Group A as Bafana Bafana were punished for their lacklustre performance.

World Cup referee headset sparks RoboCop memes during Mexico openerFIFA World Cup 2026: The football was barely minutes old when the referees stole the spotlight. The World Cup fans were left doing a double take after spotting Brazilian referee Wilton Sampaio wearing a futuristic headset that quickly sparked Xbox and RoboCop comparisons online.

Real Madrid announce Jose Mourinho as new manager to headline comeback eraReal Madrid have turned to a familiar face in their search for a revival, bringing Jose Mourinho back to the Santiago Bernabeu on a three-year deal. More than a decade after his first spell, "The Special One" is now tasked with leading another rebuild.

Mustafizur, Taskin star as Bangladesh secure maiden ODI series win vs AustraliaBangladesh beat Australia by five wickets in Dhaka to seal their first ODI series win over them. The result was sealed by a sensational early burst from Mustafizur Rahman and Taskin Ahmed with the new ball that wrecked the Aussie top order.

Shakira, Burna Boy get World Cup 2026 underway the Dai Dai wayShakira and Burna Boy opened the 2026 Men's FIFA World Cup with a live performance of "Dai Dai" at Estadio Azteca in Mexico City. The ceremony blended a tribute to Mexico's roots, a packed musical line-up and the stadium's historic third World Cup hosting role.

Highlights: South Korea and Mexico win their openers in Group AFIFA World Cup 2026 Highlights: After Mexico sealed a comprehensive win against South Africa, seven hours later, South Korea have also started their 2026 World Cup campaign with a huge victory against Czechia, coming from behind to secure three points and go second in Group A.

Germany's class act: Players fund 600 fans' trip amid transport price hikeGermany's players will cover the costs for 600 supporters to travel to their group match against Ecuador in New Jersey. The gesture was done after the travel costs in the US were spiked for the FIFA World Cup.

Barred from World Cup, Somalian referee to officiate major European fixtureOmar Abdulkadir Artan has been appointed to referee the UEFA Super Cup in Salzburg on August 12. The call comes weeks after the Somalian official missed the World Cup despite holding a valid US visa.

Manu Bhaker to spearhead India's shooting challenge at Asian GamesThe National Rifle Association of India announced a 30-member shooting contingent for the 2026 Asian Games in Aichi-Nagoya. The squad consists of as many as four double starters, including Manu Bhaker, who will compete in multiple events.

No rush on Stokes' future as England consider alcohol ban after nightclub rowRob Key is considering tougher restrictions, including a possible alcohol ban, after Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson were dropped following a London nightclub incident. Key offered no guarantees when it comes to Ben Stokes continuing as Test captain as the investigation continues.

After lucky escape vs Sri Lanka, India A lose to Afghanistan in Dambulla tri-seriesTalent tri-series, IND A vs AFG A: Afghanistan A mounted a fearless chase after rain revised India A's 349 into a 38-over target in Dambulla. Imran Mir and Bahir Shah stayed ahead of the DLS par before another downpour sealed the result on Thursday.

Men's ODI World Cup 2027 set to run from October 4 to November 21The ICC has broadly approved an October 4 to November 21 window for the 2027 Men's ODI World Cup across South Africa, Zimbabwe and Namibia. The event restores a 14-team format, returns the men's World Cup to Africa, and sits alongside wider decisions on the next FTP and WTC structure.

Grand Slam pay war: Champions to earn Rs 46 crore as Wimbledon unveils record purseWimbledon has raised its 2026 prize purse to a record £64.2 million, with singles champions set to earn £3.6 million each. The increase follows player protests over Grand Slam revenue sharing and lifts payouts across the draw.

Sooryavanshi trades aggression for restraint in 44-run cameo vs Afghanistan AVaibhav Sooryavanshi showcased a different facet of his batting during India A's clash against Afghanistan A, scoring a brisk 44 off 22 balls while relying more on timing and placement than outright power. The 15-year-old struck nine boundaries and gave India A a flying start.

Another Dravid in India colours: BCCI picks Rahul's son Anvay in U19 squadRahul Dravid's younger son, Anvay, has been named in India's Under-19 one-day squad for Sri Lanka. The call-up rewards his domestic form and begins his pathway in the next World Cup cycle.

All you need to know about FIFA World Cup opening ceremonyThe FIFA World Cup opens with three ceremonies across Mexico City, Toronto and Los Angeles over two days. Shakira will headline the first opening ceremony in Mexico on Thursday, performing her official tournament single "Dai Dai" live for the first time.

ICC annual rankings update: India remain the world's top ODI sideIndia retained the No. 1 spot in the latest ICC men's ODI team rankings following the annual update, although their advantage at the top was reduced. India now lead second-placed New Zealand by five rating points, while Australia remain third.

FIFA World Cup 2026, Group K preview: Cristiano Ronaldo's Portugal in a pickleGroup K offers a fascinating mix of established contenders and ambitious challengers. While Portugal and Colombia are expected to battle for top spot, DR Congo's resilience and Uzbekistan's defensive organisation could ensure every match carries significant weight in the race for qualification.

PV Sindhu's husband becomes an accidental coach. She has plenty to Sai about itFeeling slightly off, PV Sindhu asked husband Venkata Datta Sai to sit in her corner during her second-round match against Isharani Baruah in Sydney. She then beat her compatriot to reach the Australian Open quarter-finals on Thursday.

Australia add India-born Nikhil Chaudhary to T20Is squad for Bangladesh seriesDelhi-born all-rounder Nikhil Chaudhary could become the first Indian-born man to represent Australia in international cricket in more than 60 years after earning a call-up to the country's T20I squad for the upcoming series against Bangladesh.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.
